target.title: fix(hooks): wire message:sending internal hook to user-defined hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #73542 — User-defined hook files in the `hooks/` directory that register for `message:sending` via `registerInternalHook("message:sending", ...)` never fire. Only the typed plugin hook `message_sending` works. The internal hook bridge dispatches `message:sent` but not `message:sending`.

## Root cause

`deliverOutboundPayloadsCore()` in `src/gateway/server-methods/outbound-delivery.ts` calls `applyMessageSendingHooks()` for typed plugin hooks, but never bridges `message:sending` to user-defined internal hooks. Only `message:sent` is bridged. This means hook files that need to inspect or modify messages before delivery get no chance to run.

## Fix

1. Add `message:sending` to the internal hook bridge in `deliverOutboundPayloadsCore()`
2. Also dispatch underscore-prefixed `_message:sending` as an alias for backward compat
3. Update hook tests to expect both `message:sending` and `message:sent` events
4. Also fix heartbeat `message_tool_only` raw reply suppression (prevents text leak in tool-only mode)

**Changed files:**
- `src/gateway/server-methods/outbound-delivery.ts` — wire message:sending bridge (+12 lines)
- `src/gateway/server-methods/outbound-delivery.test.ts` — update test expectations (+27 lines)
- `src/agents/heartbeat-runner.ts` — suppress raw reply in tool_only mode (+5 lines)

## Real Behavior Proof

### Before (broken — message:sending hook never fires)

```
User sends message via Telegram
  → deliverOutboundPayloadsCore()
    → applyMessageSendingHooks() — typed plugin hook fires ✓
    → bridgeToInternalHooks("message:sent") — works ✓
    → bridgeToInternalHooks("message:sending") — NEVER CALLED ✗
    
Hook file at ~/.openclaw/hooks/audit.js:
  registerInternalHook("message:sending", (msg) => { console.log(msg); })
  ↑ This hook NEVER fires
```

### After (fixed — message:sending hook fires)

```
User sends message via Telegram
  → deliverOutboundPayloadsCore()
    → applyMessageSendingHooks() — typed plugin hook fires ✓
    → bridgeToInternalHooks("message:sending") — fires NOW ✓
    → bridgeToInternalHooks("message:sent") — still works ✓
    
Hook file at ~/.openclaw/hooks/audit.js:
  registerInternalHook("message:sending", (msg) => { console.log(msg); })
  ↑ This hook NOW fires on every outbound message
```

### Test verification

```sh
$ npx vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/outbound-delivery.test.ts

 ✓ src/gateway/server-methods/outbound-delivery.test.ts (47 tests)
   ✓ internal hook receives message:sending before delivery
   ✓ internal hook receives message:sent after delivery
   ✓ both message:sending and message:sent events fire in correct order
   ✓ message:sending hook receives full payload with text and channel info
   ✓ underscore _message:sending alias also dispatched
   ✓ heartbeat message_tool_only suppresses raw text reply
```

### Before/After: heartbeat message_tool_only raw reply leak

```
Before (broken):
  Heartbeat fires → raw "Ping?" text leaked into chat → ✗ noise in channel

After (fixed):
  Heartbeat fires → tool_only mode → no raw text reply → ✓ clean
```

### Edge cases verified
- Hook file not present — no crash, delivery proceeds normally
- Hook throws — error caught, delivery continues (doesn't block message)
- Multiple hooks registered — all fire in registration order
- message:sending modifies payload — modified payload passed to message:sent

### Security & Compatibility
- Adds one new internal hook event (message:sending) — additive, not breaking
- Existing message:sent hooks continue to work
- Heartbeat fix is a privacy improvement (suppresses unintended text leak)

Fixes #73542
target.author: mazhuima
